requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks
- Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: 1These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
requirements.object: immediate effect

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Commercial Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
requirements.object: Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
2Paragraph 18(4) of these directions shall apply to all Non-
Banking Financial Companies (including Housing Finance Companies) which
are group entities of a bank operating in India.
requirements.object: Banking Financial Companies (including Housing Finance Companies) which are group entities of a bank operating in India

requirements.full_sentence:
In these directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 3'Agency Business' means an arrangement under which a bank or its
group entity acts as an agent of a third-party product or service provider
1 Modified w.e.f.
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 3'Agency Business' means an arrangement under which a bank or its group entity acts as an agent of a thi

requirements.full_sentence:
Under agency business arrangement, the bank
and the group entity shall enter into an agreement with a TPPSP for sale
of only regulated financial products or services.
requirements.object: with a TPPSP for sale of only regulated financial products or services

requirements.full_sentence:
The activities covered
under agency business arrangements may inter-alia include marketing,
sales, promotion, initial point of contact for redressal of grievance and
other after-sale services related to the product or service
(1A) 'Assignee' shall have the same meaning as defined in the Factoring
Regulation Act, 2011;
requirements.object: as defined in the Factoring Regulation Act, 2011

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Assignor' shall have the same meaning as defined in the Factoring
Regulation Act, 2011;
requirements.object: as defined in the Factoring Regulation Act, 2011

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Associate' shall have the same meaning as defined in terms of the
Accounting Standards of the Institute of Chartered Accounts of India;
requirements.object: as defined in terms of the Accounting Standards of the Institute of Chartered Accounts of India

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) 'Debtor' shall have the same meaning as defined in the Factoring
Regulation Act, 2011;
requirements.object: as defined in the Factoring Regulation Act, 2011
the bank currently has or previously had a loan or investment exposure (excluding equity instruments) anytime during the precedin
preference shares (CCPS) and compulsorily convertible debentures (CCD)

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) 'Factoring' shall have the same meaning as defined in the Factoring
Regulation Act, 2011;
requirements.object: as defined in the Factoring Regulation Act, 2011

requirements.full_sentence:
(8) 'Financial Services Company' means a company engaged in the
'business of financial services';
requirements.object: the 'business of financial services'

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The 'business of financial services' shall include:
Note: Under referral arrangement, the bank shall neither be involved in
any of the processes relating to the third-party products or services
(TPPS) nor the name or brand of the bank shall feature in any of the
product/service documents.
requirements.object: arrangement, the bank shall neither be involved in any of the processes relating to the third-party products or services

requirements.full_sentence:
No processes relating to TPPS shall either
be integrated with the bank's platform/carried out within the premises of
the bank (unless specifically permitted) or be accessible in the form of a
micro-site or micro-application, except for an access link to redirect the
customer to the TPPSP
(18) 'Subsidiary' means a subsidiary as defined in terms of the Accounting
Standards of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India.
requirements.object: the bank's platform/carried out within the premises of the bank (unless specifically permitted) or be accessible in the form of a micro-site or micro-application

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by RBI or as used in commercial
parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, or any statutory mo

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of a bank shall be responsible for approval of policies and risk
governance in respect of various activities undertaken by the bank.
requirements.object: of policies and risk governance in respect of various activities undertaken by the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place Board-approved policies for,
(1) insurance distribution to, inter alia, ensure customer suitability,
appropriateness, a clear grievance redressal framework and
compensation policy to address service-related issues effectively;
requirements.object: policies for, (1) insurance distribution to, inter alia, ensure customer suitability, appropriateness, a clear grievance r

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall, at a minimum,
cover the model of insurance distribution to be adopted, customer
suitability and appropriateness, and the framework for grievance
redressal;
requirements.object: cover the model of insurance distribution to be adopted, customer suitability and appropriateness, and the framework for grievance red

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of insurance distribution business, the Board shall ensure that a
robust internal grievance redressal mechanism is in place.
requirements.object: internal grievance redressal mechanism is in place

conditions: Where a bank sponsors a subsidiary
requirements.full_sentence:
Where a bank sponsors a subsidiary, the Board shall ensure that an arm's length
relationship is maintained with the subsidiary.
requirements.object: length relationship is maintained with the subsidiary

requirements.full_sentence:
The performance of such
subsidiary shall be reviewed by the Board at periodic intervals.
requirements.object: Board at periodic intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of factoring business undertaken by the bank departmentally, the
Board shall approve limits for underwriting commitments pertaining to the credit
risk on the debtor under without-recourse factoring.
requirements.object: commitments pertaining to the credit risk on the debtor under without-recourse factoring

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of a bank intending to function as a Professional Clearing Member in
the commodity derivatives segment of SEBI-recognised exchanges shall:
(1) determine the aggregate exposure limits that the bank may assume on
its registered clients, in relation to the bank's net worth, and ensure that
the exposure is monitored on an ongoing basis;
requirements.object: aggregate exposure limits that the bank may assume on its registered clients, in relation to the bank's net worth, and ensure that

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) prescribe internal margin requirements for the bank's operations as a
Professional Clearing Member, which shall be at least as stringent as
those prescribed by the Commodity Exchanges and the RBI, wherever
applicable.
requirements.object: stringent as those prescribed by the Commodity Exchanges and the RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the bank's application to the RBI for obtaining a No
Objection Certificate (NOC) for its branch / subsidiary / joint venture in GIFT-
IFSC to act as a Trading Member (TM), Trading and Clearing Member (TCM),
or Professional Clearing Member (PCM) of India International Bullion Exchange
IFSC Limited (IIBX).
requirements.object: to the RBI for obtaining a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for its branch / subsidiary / joint venture in GIFT- IFSC to

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve and ensure implementation of an effective risk
management framework at the bank's branch in GIFT-IFSC, including prudential
exposure limits for each trading client based on their financial parameters such
as net worth and business turnover, shall be ensured on an ongoing basis.
requirements.object: of an effective risk management framework at the bank's branch in GIFT-IFSC, including prudential exposure limits fo

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall determine and monitor the total exposure that may be assumed
by the branch in GIFT-IFSC in its role as PCM of IIBX on its clients, in relation
to both the parent bank's and the branch's Tier 1 capital.
requirements.object: total exposure that may be assumed by the branch in GIFT-IFSC in its role as PCM of IIBX on its clients, in relation to both

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall review and approve the proposed business plan, including the
risk management architecture of the branch.
requirements.object: proposed business plan, including the risk management architecture of the branch

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall prescribe margin requirements for the bank's operations as
PCM of IIBX.
requirements.object: the bank's operations as PCM of IIBX

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board may determine a global sub-limit for the bank's proprietary net
overnight open position in gold / silver under its role as TM/TCM on IIBX, which
shall not exceed one tonne of gold equivalent.
requirements.object: gold equivalent

requirements.full_sentence:
6A bank may undertake businesses as permitted under Section 5(b) and Section
6(1) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 either departmentally or through a group
entity set up as per Section 19 of the Act, subject to the following conditions:
(1) The business of banking as defined in section 5(b) of Banking
Regulation Act, 1949 shall be undertaken only departmentally.

(2) Acceptance of time deposits shall be undertaken only departmentally by
the bank, and if permitted, through a housing finance company in the
bank group (bank group shall include bank and its group entities).
requirements.object: by the bank, and if permitted, through a housing finance company in the bank group (bank group shall include bank an

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) As a principle, any form of business shall be undertaken by one entity
in a bank group.
requirements.object: entity in a bank group

conditions:
if a bank undertakes a form of business
through more than one entity in a bank group
requirements.full_sentence:
However, if a bank undertakes a form of business
through more than one entity in a bank group, the same shall be done
with proper rationale such as business segmentation/specialization,
duly recorded and approved by the Board of the bank.
requirements.object: rationale such as business segmentation/specialization, duly recorded and approved by the Board of the bank

conditions: In case lending business is undertaken through a group entity also
requirements.full_sentence:
(4) In case lending business is undertaken through a group entity also, the
following additional conditions shall be applicable to such a group entity
(NBFCs including HFCs):
requirements.object: a group entity (NBFCs including HFCs):

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the same, listing requirement shall be
complied with by those NBFC group entities which are identified
by the Reserve Bank as Upper Layer.
requirements.object: those NBFC group entities which are identified by the Reserve Bank as Upper Layer

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Furthermore, following stipulations/restrictions under Reserve
Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Risk Management)
Directions, 2025 and Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks
- Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025 shall apply mutatis-
mutandis to all the fresh/renewal and additional loans/limits
sanctioned/disbursed by the NBFC group entities:
6 Amended w.e.f December 05, 2025, vide Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Undertaking of Financial
Services) (Amendment) Directions, 2025
<image>
requirements.object: all the fresh/renewal and additional loans/limits sanctioned/disbursed by the NBFC group entities: 6 Amended w

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) 8[Lending to individuals against eligible securities]
(5) Mutual fund business, insurance business, pension fund management,
investment advisory and management services, portfolio management
services and broking services shall not be carried out departmentally,
but, only through a group entity.
requirements.object: but, only through a group entity

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) A bank shall require prior approval of the Reserve Bank to undertake
any new form of business through the bank or a group entity, other than
those permitted in these directions.
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank to undertake any new form of business through the bank or a group entity, other than those permitted in the

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) There shall be a policy for each form of business undertaken by the
bank, which comprehensively covers various aspects of the said
business including identification of various risks associated with it, an
appropriate risk mitigation framework, and adherence to capital
allocation norms as applicable.
requirements.object: each form of business undertaken by the bank, which comprehensively covers various aspects of the said business including identificati

requirements.full_sentence:
9A bank which is not in conformity with the directions given in the paragraph 18
above, shall not undertake any new business in the respective segment from April
1, 2026.
requirements.object: in the respective segment from April 1, 2026

requirements.full_sentence: It shall also submit a compliance status in this regard by March 31, 2026.
requirements.object: status in this regard by March 31, 2026

requirements.full_sentence: Existing facilities, however, shall continue to be serviced till its maturity.
requirements.object: till its maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
10An NOFHC shall not require prior approval of the Reserve Bank for the entities
held by it to undertake forms of businesses listed under para 18(5) above unless
advised otherwise.
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank for the entities held by it to undertake forms of businesses listed under para 18(5) above unless advised o

requirements.timeline:
within 15 days
from the date of resolution of the Board for undertaking such businesses
requirements.full_sentence:
The NOFHC shall intimate the Reserve Bank within 15 days
from the date of resolution of the Board for undertaking such businesses.
requirements.object: within 15 days from the date of resolution of the Board for undertaking such businesses

requirements.full_sentence:
Further,
an NOFHC shall require the prior approval of the Reserve Bank for the entities
held by it to undertake any other form of business subject to other instructions as
applicable.
requirements.object: of the Reserve Bank for the entities held by it to undertake any other form of business subject to other instructions as ap

requirements.full_sentence:
11Unless specifically permitted, a bank shall obtain no-objection from the
Reserve Bank for any of its overseas branches (including the branches
operating in IFSC, GIFT City) to undertake any business for which prior approval
is required by the parent bank in India as per para 18(6) above.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank for any of its overseas branches (including the branches operating in IFSC, GIFT City) to undertake any busi

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, a bank
shall ensure that its overseas branches (including the branches operating in
IFSC, GIFT City) adhere to the more stringent of the host or home country
regulations for the purpose of this Master Direction.
requirements.object: branches (including the branches operating in IFSC, GIFT City) adhere to the more stringent of the host or home country regul

requirements.full_sentence:
12Investment by a bank in any group entity or in other entities, including overseas
investments, shall be subject to the prudential limits specified below:
B.1 13Limits in terms of bank's paid-up share capital and reserves
23.
requirements.object: prudential limits specified below: B

requirements.full_sentence:
Equity investment by a bank in any entity, including its group entity, individually,
shall not exceed 10 per cent of the bank's paid-up share capital and reserves as
per the last audited balance sheet or audited/unaudited balance sheet of the
latest quarter, whichever is lower.
requirements.object: of the bank's paid-up share capital and reserves as per the last audited balance sheet or audited/unaudited balance sheet of the la

requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate equity investments made in all entities, including group entities
and overseas investments, shall not exceed 20 per cent of the bank's paid-up
share capital and reserves as per the last audited balance sheet or
audited/unaudited balance sheet of the latest quarter, whichever is lower.
requirements.object: of the bank's paid-up share capital and reserves as per the last audited balance sheet or audited/unaudited balance sheet of the la

requirements.full_sentence:
For
calculating the aggregate equity investment limit, the following investments shall
be excluded.

A bank group may make an aggregate investment of less than 20 per cent (with
or without investment by the bank) in the equity share capital of an entity without
prior approval, subject to the following conditions:
(1) The bank's CRAR shall not be less than the minimum prescribed capital
(including Capital Conservation Buffer) post the investment;
requirements.object: minimum prescribed capital (including Capital Conservation Buffer) post the investment

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) The bank should have reported net profit in each of the preceding two financial
years.
requirements.object: in each of the preceding two financial years

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the conditions listed in para 25, investments held under 'Held
for Trading' category shall not require prior approval, subject to the limit
stipulated under Section 19(2) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.

Investments made by the Asset Management Companies (AMCs) from the
funds of the unit holders, shall not be included in the aggregate limit for the
purpose of para 25 and para 28.
requirements.object: aggregate limit for the purpose of para 25 and para 28

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the investment made by group
14 Amended w.e.f December 05, 2025, vide Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Undertaking of Financial
Services) (Amendment) Directions, 2025
<image>
entities of the bank including AMCs, from their own funds, shall be included for
the purpose of the aggregate limit of the bank group.
requirements.object: purpose of the aggregate limit of the bank group

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank group, with prior approval of the Reserve Bank, may make an aggregate
investment of 20 per cent or more (with or without any investment by the bank)
in the equity share capital of an entity.
requirements.object: of 20 per cent or more (with or without any investment by the bank) in the equity share capital of an entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks shall be allowed to set up subsidiaries only for undertaking activities as
permitted under Section 19(1) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: up subsidiaries only for undertaking activities as permitted under Section 19(1) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

requirements.full_sentence:
Further,
banks shall require prior approval for making additional investment in the equity
share capital of any group entity.
requirements.object: making additional investment in the equity share capital of any group entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Save as otherwise provided in these directions, the following specific
limits/conditions shall be adhered to:
(1) A bank shall be allowed to invest 20 per cent or more in the equity share
capital of a non-financial services entity but not exceeding 30 per cent
(except as subsidiary) only in the following circumstances:
requirements.object: A bank shall be allowed to invest 20 per cent or more in the equity share capital of a non-financial services entity but not exceed

requirements.full_sentence: (iii) Banks shall not require prior approval for such acquisitions;
requirements.object: such acquisitions

requirements.full_sentence:
however,
they shall submit a time bound action plan for disposal of such
shares.
requirements.object: action plan for disposal of such shares

requirements.full_sentence:
The action plan shall be submitted within 30 days of such
acquisition.
requirements.object: days of such acquisition

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall not sponsor (as defined in the Securitisation and
Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest
Act, 2002) more than one Asset Reconstruction Company (ARC).
requirements.object: the Securitisation and Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest Act, 2002) more than one Asset R

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the aggregate shareholding of a bank group in any ARC shall be
less than 20 per cent of the equity share capital of the ARC.
requirements.object: per cent of the equity share capital of the ARC

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Banks held under the NOFHC structure shall adhere to Guidelines for
Licensing of New Banks in the Private Sector' dated February 22, 2013,
for their investments in the equity share capital of a company.
requirements.object: Licensing of New Banks in the Private Sector' dated February 22, 2013, for their investments in the equity share capital of a

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank's investment policy shall have suitable provisions governing its
investments in an AIF Scheme, compliant with extant law and regulations.
requirements.object: its investments in an AIF Scheme, compliant with extant law and regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
15A bank shall not individually contribute more than 10 percent of the corpus of
a Category I or Category II AIF Scheme.
requirements.object: 10 percent of the corpus of a Category I or Category II AIF Scheme

requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate contribution by all Regulated Entities (REs) in any AIF Scheme
shall not be more than 20 percent of the corpus of that scheme.
requirements.object: percent of the corpus of that scheme

requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, 'RE' shall mean:
(1) Commercial Banks (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks);
requirements.object: (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks)

conditions:
IF Scheme
that has downstream investment (excluding equity instruments) in a debtor
company of the bank
requirements.full_sentence:
Where a bank contributes more than five percent of the corpus of an AIF Scheme
that has downstream investment (excluding equity instruments) in a debtor
company of the bank, the bank shall be required to make 100 percent provision
to the extent of its proportionate investment in the debtor company through the
AIF Scheme, subject to a cap equivalent to bank's direct loan and / or investment
exposure to the said debtor company.
requirements.object: 100 percent provision to the extent of its proportionate investment in the debtor company through the AIF Scheme

conditions:
where a bank's
contribution is in the form of subordinated units
requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph 33 above, where a bank's
contribution is in the form of subordinated units, it shall deduct the entire
investment from its capital funds - proportionately from both Tier-1 and Tier-2
capital (wherever applicable).
requirements.object: from its capital funds - proportionately from both Tier-1 and Tier-2 capital (wherever applicable)

conditions: IF contained in Part B.3 of Chapter III
requirements.full_sentence:
17The RBI may, in consultation with the Government of India, by way of a
notification, exempt certain AIFs (Annex-I) from the scope of these Directions on
AIF contained in Part B.3 of Chapter III, except for paragraph 30 above.
requirements.object: the Government of India, by way of a notification, exempt certain AIFs (Annex-I) from the scope of these Directions on AIF contained in Part B.3 of Chapter III

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank group may make an investment of less than 20 per cent in the corpus of
Category I or Category II AIF scheme, without prior approval subject to conditions
stipulated under paragraph 25 above.
requirements.object: less than 20 per cent in the corpus of Category I or Category II AIF scheme, without prior approval subject to conditions stipul

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank group may make an investment of 20 per cent or more but not exceeding
30% in the corpus of Category I or II AIF scheme, with prior approval of the
Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: 20 per cent or more but not exceeding 30% in the corpus of Category I or II AIF scheme, with prior approval of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence: No bank shall make any investment in the corpus of Category III AIF scheme.
requirements.object: the corpus of Category III AIF scheme

requirements.full_sentence:
Investment by a bank's subsidiary in the corpus of Category III AIF scheme shall
also be restricted to the regulatory minima prescribed by the Securities and
Exchange Board of India.
requirements.object: the regulatory minima prescribed by the Securities and Exchange Board of India

requirements.full_sentence:
Additionally, banks shall ensure that their exposure in an investee company
through their investments in AIF schemes does not result in circumvention of any
regulations applicable to banks.
requirements.object: in an investee company through their investments in AIF schemes does not result in circumvention of any regulations applica

requirements.full_sentence:
No bank shall make an investment of more than 10 per cent in the unit capital of
a Real Estate Investment Trust/Infrastructure Investment Trust within the overall
ceiling of 20 per cent of the bank's net worth permitted for all direct investments
in shares, convertible bonds/ debentures, units of equity-oriented mutual funds
and exposures to AIFs.
requirements.object: more than 10 per cent in the unit capital of a Real Estate Investment Trust/Infrastructure Investment Trust within the overall c

requirements.full_sentence:
As part of bank's ICAAP framework, the following shall be accounted for the
purpose of determining additional capital requirement which will be subject to
Supervisory Review and Evaluation Process:
(1) risks arising on account of equity investments in unit/paid-up capital, as
applicable, of an AIF Scheme done directly or through their group entities.
requirements.object: purpose of determining additional capital requirement which will be subject to Supervisory Review and Evaluation Process: (1) ris

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be read in conjunction with Reserve Bank of India
(Commercial Banks - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025 as applicable
to banks.
requirements.object: with Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025 as applicable to banks

requirements.full_sentence:
Breach, if any, in the limits prescribed under this Master Direction shall be
reported to the Department of Regulation of the Reserve Bank on PRAVAAH portal
through the applicable form within 15 days, from the date of occurrence of such breach
along with reason for such a breach and a plan to correct the same.
requirements.object: Department of Regulation of the Reserve Bank on PRAVAAH portal through the applicable form within 15 days, from the date of occurre

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank which is not in conformity with the directions given in paragraphs 22 to 29A
and 38A to 38E above, shall submit an action plan by March 31, 2026, to comply with
the provisions contained herein within a specified timeline but not later than March 31,
2028.
requirements.object: by March 31, 2026, to comply with the provisions contained herein within a specified timeline but not later than March 31, 2028

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank desirous of making an investment or undertaking any business that
requires prior approval / no-objection of RBI shall submit an application through
PRAVAAH Portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in) to the Department of Regulation
(DoR), Central Office, RBI.
requirements.object: PRAVAAH Portal (https://pravaah

requirements.full_sentence:
A parent bank shall maintain an 'arm's length' relationship with the subsidiary
sponsored by it and evolve the following supervisory strategies:
(1) The Board of the parent bank shall review the working of subsidiaries at
periodical intervals;
requirements.object: relationship with the subsidiary sponsored by it and evolve the following supervisory strategies: (1) The Board of the pare

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The parent bank shall undertake inspection / audit of the books of
accounts of the subsidiaries at periodical intervals;
requirements.object: of the books of accounts of the subsidiaries at periodical intervals
or promote a new company which is not a subsidiary thereof or undertake any new business without prior approval of RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: 'New Business' shall not mean expansion into the same line
of business that is already permitted / approved to be undertaken.
requirements.object: same line of business that is already permitted / approved to be undertaken
in another existing company with an intention of acquiring controlling interest, without prior approval of the RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: This shall not apply to the investments made by a Category I
and II AIF set up by a subsidiary.
requirements.object: made by a Category I and II AIF set up by a subsidiary

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) A subsidiary shall not have any on-line access to customers' accounts
maintained with the bank.
requirements.object: to customers' accounts maintained with the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The information between a bank and its
subsidiary may be shared subject to maintaining arm's length relationship;
requirements.object: maintaining arm's length relationship

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The bank shall not grant any unsecured advances to the subsidiary
without prior approval of the RBI;
requirements.object: to the subsidiary without prior approval of the RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(7) Transactions between a bank and its subsidiary shall be at arm's length.

No preferential treatment shall be given to the subsidiary vis-à-vis a
counterparty with similar risk characteristics
requirements.object: subsidiary vis-à-vis a counterparty with similar risk characteristics

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Credit information regarding the non-payment of dues by the person on
whom exposure was booked shall be furnished to the Credit Information
Companies authorized by RBI subject to the guidelines under Credit
Information Companies (Regulation) Act, 2005;
requirements.object: Credit Information Companies authorized by RBI subject to the guidelines under Credit Information Companies (Regulation) Act, 2005

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(8) The exposure for facilities extended by way of factoring services shall be
reckoned as under:

A bank intending to form a subsidiary for undertaking equipment leasing and hire
purchase business shall be subject to the conditions mentioned in paragraph 22.
requirements.object: conditions mentioned in paragraph 22

requirements.full_sentence:
Equipment Leasing and Hire Purchase business undertaken departmentally
shall be subject to the following conditions:
(1) Equipment leasing and hire purchase shall be treated on par with loans
and advances and shall accordingly be subject to the extant prudential
norms on loans and advances as applicable;
requirements.object: following conditions: (1) Equipment leasing and hire purchase shall be treated on par with loans and advances and shall accordingly

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) A bank shall not enter into leasing agreement with another equipment
leasing company and other non-banking finance company engaged in
equipment leasing.
requirements.object: with another equipment leasing company and other non-banking finance company engaged in equipment leasing

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank intending to form a subsidiary for undertaking factoring business shall be
subject to the conditions mentioned in paragraph 22.
requirements.object: conditions mentioned in paragraph 22

requirements.full_sentence:
Factoring business undertaken departmentally shall be subject to the following
conditions:
(1) Factoring services shall be provided on with recourse or without recourse
or on limited recourse basis;
requirements.object: following conditions: (1) Factoring services shall be provided on with recourse or without recourse or on limited recourse basis
the Board approved limits
of debtors before entering into any factoring arrangement or establishing lines of credit with the export factor

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Factoring services shall be extended against invoices representing
genuine trade transactions;
requirements.object: representing genuine trade transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(5) Factoring shall be treated on par with loans and advances and shall be
subject to extant prudential norms on loans and advances as applicable;
requirements.object: with loans and advances and shall be subject to extant prudential norms on loans and advances as applicable

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) A bank and factor shall put in place a mechanism for sharing information
about common borrowers to avoid double financing.
requirements.object: mechanism for sharing information about common borrowers to avoid double financing

requirements.full_sentence:
The borrower's bank
shall obtain periodical certificates from the borrower about factored
receivables.
requirements.object: the borrower about factored receivables

requirements.full_sentence:
Factors shall report the sanctioned limits of the borrower to
the concerned banks.
requirements.object: of the borrower to the concerned banks

requirements.full_sentence:
Information available on CERSAI shall also be
considered;

A bank intending to form a subsidiary for undertaking primary dealership
business shall register it as an NBFC.
requirements.object: NBFC

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall directly approach
Department of Regulation (DoR), RBI for the same.
requirements.object: Regulation (DoR), RBI for the same

requirements.full_sentence:
Primary dealership business undertaken departmentally shall be subject to the
authorisation from the Internal Debt Management Department (IDMD), RBI.
requirements.object: authorisation from the Internal Debt Management Department (IDMD), RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The
bank shall directly approach IDMD for the same.
requirements.object: the same

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank intending to engage in underwriting of issues of shares, debentures and
bonds shall do so either departmentally or through a merchant banking subsidiary.
requirements.object: or through a merchant banking subsidiary

requirements.full_sentence:
Underwriting business undertaken departmentally and through subsidiary shall
be subject to the conditions and prudential regulation requirements specified in
requirements.object: conditions and prudential regulation requirements specified in

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall undertake mutual fund business with risk participation only through
a subsidiary / joint venture set up for the purpose.
requirements.object: with risk participation only through a subsidiary / joint venture set up for the purpose

conditions:
Where a sponsoring bank undertaking the mutual fund business lends its name
to the bank sponsored mutual fund
requirements.full_sentence:
Where a sponsoring bank undertaking the mutual fund business lends its name
to the bank sponsored mutual fund, a suitable disclaimer clause shall be inserted
while publicising new schemes to the effect that the bank is not liable or
responsible for any loss or shortfall resulting from the operations of the scheme.
requirements.object: new schemes to the effect that the bank is not liable or responsible for any loss or shortfall resulting from the operat

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall undertake insurance business with risk participation only through a
subsidiary / joint venture set up for the purpose, and subject to fulfilment of the
following eligibility criteria as on March 31 of the previous year:
(1) It has a net worth of at least ₹1000 crore and a minimum net worth of
₹500 crore after investing in the equity of the insurance company;
requirements.object: risk participation only through a subsidiary / joint venture set up for the purpose, and subject to fulfilment of th

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall set up a subsidiary / joint venture company for undertaking
insurance broking and corporate agency only after fulfilling the following
eligibility criteria as on March 31 of the previous year:
(1) Its net worth shall not be less than ₹500 crore after investing in the equity
of such company;
requirements.object: crore after investing in the equity of such company

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may, at its option, act as an insurance broker departmentally subject to
the conditions mentioned under paragraph 61 on insurance agency business
requirements.object: act as an insurance broker departmentally subject to the conditions mentioned under paragraph 61 on insurance agency business

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall undertake the business of pension fund management only through
a subsidiary set up for the purpose, subject to the fulfilment of the following
eligibility criteria as on March 31 of the previous year:
(1) Its net worth shall not be less than ₹500 crore after investing in the
equity of such company;
requirements.object: crore after investing in the equity of such company

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall undertake the business of investment advisory services (IAS) only
through a separate subsidiary set up for the purpose or through one of its
existing subsidiaries, subject to the following conditions:
(1) Specific prior approval shall be obtained before offering IAS;
requirements.object: investment advisory services (IAS) only through a separate subsidiary set up for the purpose or through one of its existing subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) IAS shall be provided only for products and services in which banks
are permitted to deal in as per the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: products and services in which banks are permitted to deal in as per the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

requirements.full_sentence:
22Banks shall offer portfolio management service (PMS) or similar scheme only
through a group entity subject to prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: (PMS) or similar scheme only through a group entity subject to prior approval of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure compliance with the following conditions:
(1) PMS shall be in the nature of investment consultancy / management, for
a fee, entirely at the customer's risk without guaranteeing, either directly
or indirectly, a pre-determined return;
requirements.object: following conditions: (1) PMS shall be in the nature of investment consultancy / management, for a fee, entirely at the cus

requirements.full_sentence: (2) The fee charged shall be independent of the return to the client;
requirements.object: return to the client

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The funds shall not be accepted for portfolio management for a period less
than one year.
requirements.object: management for a period less than one year

requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of placement of funds for portfolio management
by the same client on more than one occasion, on a continuous basis,
each such placement shall be treated as a separate account and each
such placement shall be for a minimum period of one year;
requirements.object: separate account and each such placement shall be for a minimum period of one year

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Funds accepted for portfolio management from the clients, shall not be
entrusted to another bank for management;
requirements.object: bank for management

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Portfolio funds shall not be deployed for lending in call money / bills
market, and lending to / placement with corporate bodies;
requirements.object: in call money / bills market, and lending to / placement with corporate bodies

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) A bank providing PMS shall maintain client wise account / record of funds
accepted and investments made there against, and all credits (including
realised interest, dividend, etc.) and debits (tax deducted at source in
respect of interest / dividend on securities) shall reflect in such account.
requirements.object: / record of funds accepted and investments made there against, and all credits (including realised interest, dividend, et

requirements.full_sentence:
The account holder shall be entitled to get a statement of his portfolio
account;
requirements.object: a statement of his portfolio account

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) A bank's own investments and investments belonging to the PMS clients
shall be kept distinct from each other.
requirements.object: each other

requirements.full_sentence:
Transactions between the bank's
investment account and portfolio account shall be strictly at market rates;
requirements.object: rates

requirements.full_sentence:
(8) A bank shall maintain a 'Clients' Portfolio Account' in its general ledger,
reflecting the funds received by it for portfolio management on a day-to-
day basis.
requirements.object: Account' in its general ledger, reflecting the funds received by it for portfolio management on a day-to- day basis

requirements.full_sentence:
undeployed funds, if any,
from this account) shall be treated as outside borrowings of the bank and
22 Amended w.e.f December 05, 2025, vide Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Undertaking of Financial
Services) (Amendment) Directions, 2025
<image>
it shall maintain cash reserve ratio / statutory liquidity ratio on such funds.
requirements.object: borrowings of the bank and 22 Amended w
the bank's published books of accounts
separation of trading and back-office functions relating to banks' own investment accounts and PMS clients' accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(10) PMS clients' accounts shall be subjected by banks to a separate audit by
external auditors.
requirements.object: to a separate audit by external auditors

requirements.full_sentence:
The aforesaid conditions shall, mutatis mutandis, be applicable to the
subsidiaries of a bank in so far as they are not contradictory to specific
regulations of RBI or SEBI, governing their operations.
requirements.object: applicable to the subsidiaries of a bank in so far as they are not contradictory to specific regulations of RBI or SEBI, govern

requirements.full_sentence:
Agency business shall be undertaken only for the products and services in which
a bank is permitted to deal in as per the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: the products and services in which a bank is permitted to deal in as per the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

requirements.full_sentence: The service shall be provided on fee basis, without any risk participation.
requirements.object: basis, without any risk participation

requirements.full_sentence:
Agency business of mutual fund companies undertaken departmentally shall be
subject to the following additional conditions:
(1) The investors' applications for purchase / sale of mutual fund units shall
be forwarded to the mutual funds / registrars / transfer agents;
requirements.object: following additional conditions: (1) The investors' applications for purchase / sale of mutual fund units shall be forwarded to the

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The purchase of units shall be at the customers' risk without the bank
guaranteeing any assured return;
requirements.object: risk without the bank guaranteeing any assured return

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Mutual fund units shall not be acquired from the secondary market or
bought back from a customer for selling it to other customers;
requirements.object: secondary market or bought back from a customer for selling it to other customers
the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(5) A bank holding custody of mutual fund units on behalf of its customers
shall keep the investments of the customers distinct from its own
investments.
requirements.object: the customers distinct from its own investments

requirements.full_sentence:
Corporate agency of insurance companies undertaken departmentally by banks
shall be subject to the following additional conditions:
<image>
(1) There shall be a Board approved policy encompassing the model of
insurance distribution to be adopted, issues of customer appropriateness,
suitability, and grievance redressal;
requirements.object: following additional conditions: <image> (1) There shall be a Board approved policy encompassing the model of insurance distribution

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The deposit to be maintained by an insurance broker as per the IRDAI
(Licensing of Banks as Insurance Brokers) Regulations, 2013, as
amended from time to time, shall be maintained with a scheduled
commercial bank other than itself;
requirements.object: scheduled commercial bank other than itself

requirements.full_sentence: (3) A bank shall ensure customer appropriateness and suitability as under:
requirements.object: suitability as under:

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) All employees dealing with insurance agency / broking business shall
possess the requisite qualification prescribed by IRDAI;
requirements.object: prescribed by IRDAI

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) There shall be standardised system of assessing the need/ suitability
of products for a customer and the initiation / transaction processes
shall be segregated.
requirements.object: assessing the need/ suitability of products for a customer and the initiation / transaction processes shall be segregated

requirements.full_sentence:
Products with investment components shall
require the bank to necessarily undertake a customer need
assessment prior to sale whereas pure risk term products with no
investment or growth component shall be deemed as universally
suitable products;
requirements.object: necessarily undertake a customer need assessment prior to sale whereas pure risk term products with no investment or growth compon

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) A bank shall treat its customers fairly, honestly and transparently, with
regard to suitability and appropriateness of the insurance product
sold.
requirements.object: honestly and transparently, with regard to suitability and appropriateness of the insurance product sold

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) It shall be ensured that performance assessment and incentive structure
for staff is not violative of Section 10(1) (ii) of the BR Act, 1949 or the
guidelines issued by IRDAI in payment of commissions / brokerage /
incentives.
requirements.object: assessment and incentive structure for staff is not violative of Section 10(1) (ii) of the BR Act, 1949 or the guidelines
no incentive (cash or non-cash) is paid to the staff engaged in insurance broking / corporate agency services by the insurance co

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) A bank shall not follow any restrictive practices of forcing a customer to
either opt for products of a specific insurance company or link sale of such
products to any banking product.
requirements.object: of forcing a customer to either opt for products of a specific insurance company or link sale of such products to any

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall be prominently stated in all
publicity material distributed by the bank that the purchase by a bank's
customer of any insurance product is purely voluntary and is not linked to
availment of any other facility from the bank;
requirements.object: all publicity material distributed by the bank that the purchase by a bank's customer of any insurance product is purely volu

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(6) A robust internal grievance redressal mechanism shall be put in place
along with a Board approved customer compensation policy for resolving
<image>
issues related to services offered.
requirements.object: along with a Board approved customer compensation policy for resolving <image> issues related to services offered

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall be ensured that the insurance
companies whose products are being sold have robust customer
grievance redressal arrangements in place.
requirements.object: insurance companies whose products are being sold have robust customer grievance redressal arrangements in place

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall facilitate the
redressal of grievances.
requirements.object: grievances

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank offering referral services shall do so only for financial products other than
insurance, on a non-risk participation basis.
requirements.object: financial products other than insurance, on a non-risk participation basis

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank intending to undertake the business of retailing of Government Securities
shall do so with non-bank clients subject to the Directions issued by RBI on the
subject.
requirements.object: clients subject to the Directions issued by RBI on the subject

requirements.full_sentence:
An AD Category I scheduled commercial bank may become a trading / clearing
member of the currency derivatives segment of the SEBI recognised stock
exchanges, subject to meeting all of the following conditions:
(1) It has a minimum net worth of ₹500 crore;
requirements.object: clearing member of the currency derivatives segment of the SEBI recognised stock exchanges

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, a bank not meeting the aforesaid conditions may participate in the
currency futures market as a client.
requirements.object: meeting the aforesaid conditions may participate in the currency futures market as a client

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank that is a trading / clearing member shall keep its and its clients'
position distinct from one another.
requirements.object: clients' position distinct from one another

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank which intends to become a member of a SEBI approved stock exchange
for the purpose of undertaking proprietary transactions in the corporate bond
market shall do so subject to satisfying the membership criteria of the stock
exchanges and complying with the regulatory norms laid down by SEBI and the
respective stock exchange.
requirements.object: satisfying the membership criteria of the stock exchanges and complying with the regulatory norms laid down by SEBI and the respectiv

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may become a Professional Clearing Member of the commodity
derivatives segment of SEBI recognised exchanges only upon satisfying the
prudential criteria given in paragraph 64, and subject to the following conditions:
(1) A bank shall satisfy the membership criteria of the stock exchanges and
comply with the regulatory norms laid down by SEBI and the respective
stock exchanges;
requirements.object: of the stock exchanges and comply with the regulatory norms laid down by SEBI and the respective stock exchanges

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall, with the approval of Board, put in place effective risk control
measures, prudential norms on risk exposure in respect of each of its
trading members, taking into account their net worth, business turnover,
etc.;
requirements.object: of Board, put in place effective risk control measures, prudential norms on risk exposure in respect of each of its trading member

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall not undertake trading in the derivative segment of the
commodity exchange on its own account and shall restrict itself only to
clearing and settlement transactions done by the trading members / clients
on the exchange;
requirements.object: derivative segment of the commodity exchange on its own account and shall restrict itself only to clearing and settlement tra

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall take exposure on its trading members as per the policy
approved by its board;
requirements.object: trading members as per the policy approved by its board

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) A bank may fulfil pay-in obligations arising out of trades executed by its
clients, as clearing member of the exchange subject to the condition that
the total exposure which the bank would take on its registered clients
should be determined by the Board in relation to the net worth of the bank
and should be monitored regularly.
requirements.object: out of trades executed by its clients, as clearing member of the exchange subject to the condition that the total ex
of any transaction other than what is required in its role as a Professional Clearing Member

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(6) A bank shall ensure strict compliance with various margin requirements
as may be prescribed by a bank's board or the Commodity Exchanges as
also the extant RBI guidelines regarding guarantees issued on behalf of
commodity brokers.
requirements.object: various margin requirements as may be prescribed by a bank's board or the Commodity Exchanges as also the extant RBI gui

requirements.full_sentence:
23A bank may become Professional Clearing Member for equity derivative
segment of the SEBI recognised stock exchanges.
requirements.object: for equity derivative segment of the SEBI recognised stock exchanges

requirements.full_sentence:
Prudential criteria and
23 Amended w.e.f December 05, 2025, vide Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Undertaking of Financial
Services) (Amendment) Directions, 2025
<image>
conditions stipulated for commodity derivative segment in para 66 above shall
apply mutatis mutandis to the equity derivative segment.
requirements.object: the equity derivative segment

requirements.full_sentence:
24No bank shall offer broking services for the commodity derivatives segment of
SEBI recognised stock exchanges except through a separate subsidiary, subject
to the following conditions:
(1) The subsidiary shall, with the approval of its Board, put in place effective
risk control measures including prudential norms on risk exposure in
respect of each of its clients, taking into account their net worth, business
turnover, etc.
requirements.object: the commodity derivatives segment of SEBI recognised stock exchanges except through a separate subsidiary

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The subsidiary shall not undertake proprietary positions in the commodity
derivatives segments.
requirements.object: the commodity derivatives segments

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The subsidiary shall ensure strict compliance with various margin
requirements as may be prescribed by SEBI, its own board or the
Commodity Exchanges.
requirements.object: various margin requirements as may be prescribed by SEBI, its own board or the Commodity Exchanges

requirements.full_sentence:
Branch of a bank in GIFT-IFSC may act as a PCM of IIBX, subject to the parent
bank ('bank') obtaining a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the RBI.
requirements.object: of IIBX

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall ensure strict compliance on a continuing basis with the following
terms and conditions for operating as a PCM of IIBX:
(1) The bank shall ensure adherence to extant RBI guidelines on capital
requirements for their exposures (including but not limited to default fund
contributions, posted collateral, exposure to clients, trade exposure to
CCP) arising from its branch in GIFT-IFSC functioning as PCM on IIBX.
requirements.object: a continuing basis with the following terms and conditions for operating as a PCM of IIBX: (1) The bank shall ensure adher

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall comply with the regulatory capital requirement of the host
or home regulator, whichever is more stringent;
requirements.object: capital requirement of the host or home regulator, whichever is more stringent

requirements.full_sentence:
24 Amended w.e.f December 05, 2025, vide Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Undertaking of Financial
Services) (Amendment) Directions, 2025
<image>
(2) The bank shall ensure adherence to extant RBI guidelines on
management of liquidity risk (including those arising from its functioning as
a PCM of IIBX) as issued from time to time;
requirements.object: RBI guidelines on management of liquidity risk (including those arising from its functioning as a PCM of IIBX) as issued fr
extant RBI guidelines on large exposure framework as issued from time to time, including all exposures taken by its branch in

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) In line with the extant prudential regulations applicable to the bank, its
branch in GIFT-IFSC shall, with the approval of the bank's Board, put in
place an effective risk management framework including the prudential
limits in respect of each of its trading clients, taking into account their net
worth, business turnover, and other relevant parameters as per the bank's
assessment.
requirements.object: of the bank's Board, put in place an effective risk management framework including the prudential limits in respect of each of its
the guidelines / Directions issued by the host or home regulator, whichever is more stringent

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The branch of the bank in GIFT-IFSC may, as a PCM of IIBX, clear and
settle trades executed by its clients as trading members of the exchanges
subject to the condition that the total exposure which the branch would
take on its clients shall be determined by the Board in relation to the Tier
1 capital of the bank as well as the capital of its branch in GIFT-IFSC and
shall be monitored on an ongoing basis.
requirements.object: Board in relation to the Tier 1 capital of the bank as well as the capital of its branch in GIFT-IFSC and shall be monitored on a

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the bank shall ensure
that its branch in GIFT-IFSC, in its role as a PCM, does not undertake any
transaction / activity on IIBX other than what is required as a professional
clearing member;
requirements.object: in GIFT-IFSC, in its role as a PCM, does not undertake any transaction / activity on IIBX other than what is required as a prof

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The bank shall ensure strict compliance with various margin requirements
as may be prescribed by its Board;
requirements.object: various margin requirements as may be prescribed by its Board

conditions:
if any, stipulated by other
regulatory bodies that may be relevant for its role as a PCM
requirements.full_sentence:
(7) The bank shall comply with all the conditions, if any, stipulated by other
regulatory bodies that may be relevant for its role as a PCM;
requirements.object: conditions

conditions:
if the RBI is
satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest so to do
requirements.full_sentence:
and
(8) In the event of non-compliance with extant guidelines, or if the RBI is
satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest so to do,
it may issue further necessary Directions (including revocation of approval)
and / or impose additional conditions, as it deems fit.
requirements.object: (including revocation of approval) and / or impose additional conditions, as it deems fit

requirements.full_sentence:
Branch / subsidiary / joint venture of a bank in GIFT-IFSC may act as a Trading
Member (TM) / Trading and Clearing Member (TCM) of IIBX, subject to the
parent bank ('bank') obtaining a NOC from the RBI.
requirements.object: Member (TM) / Trading and Clearing Member (TCM) of IIBX

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall ensure strict compliance on a continuing basis with the following
terms and conditions for operating as TM / TCM of IIBX:
(1) The TM / TCM shall execute trades only on behalf of clients (without
proprietary trading).
requirements.object: a continuing basis with the following terms and conditions for operating as TM / TCM of IIBX: (1) The TM / TCM shall execu

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The bank in its role as TM / TCM shall ensure adherence to the terms and
conditions stipulated under paragraph 69 above.
requirements.object: terms and conditions stipulated under paragraph 69 above

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The bank shall ensure adherence to extant RBI instructions as contained
in the Master Direction - Credit Facility (as updated from time to time).
requirements.object: RBI instructions as contained in the Master Direction - Credit Facility (as updated from time to time)

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) All client trades placed on the exchange shall be against 110 percent
advance pay-in of funds (buy order) of the expected value of bullion
(quantity & quality specification) intended to be purchased and securities
(sell order) in the account of the bank, as is applicable.
requirements.object: advance pay-in of funds (buy order) of the expected value of bullion (quantity & quality specification) intended to be purchase

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) With reference to the Net Open Overnight Position Limit (NOOPL) for a
bank as prescribed in Master Direction - Risk Management and Inter-Bank
Dealings dated July 05, 2016 (as updated from time to time), the Board
may determine a global sub-limit for net overnight open position in gold /
silver, which shall not exceed one tonne of gold equivalent.
requirements.object: gold equivalent

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The bank shall comply with the conditions, if any, stipulated by other
regulatory bodies that may be relevant for its role.

Banks authorized by the RBI for import of gold / silver as per extant Foreign
Trade Policy, in addition to the consignment model in domestic tariff area, are
allowed to operate as a Special Category Client (SCC) on IIBX for import of gold
/ silver by sending prior intimation to the Department of Regulation, RBI, and
subject to the following conditions:
(1) The SCC shall execute only buy trades on behalf of clients;
requirements.object: on behalf of clients

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) The bank shall ensure adherence to extant RBI instructions as contained
in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities)
Directions, 2025 (as updated from time to time);
requirements.object: RBI instructions as contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025 (as upda

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The SCCs shall adhere to the conditions stipulated under clauses (4) and
(5) of paragraph 71 above;
requirements.object: stipulated under clauses (4) and (5) of paragraph 71 above

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(5) The bank shall comply with the conditions, if any, stipulated by other
regulatory bodies that may be relevant for its role.

The parent bank shall, with prior approval of its Board, make an application for
NOC for PCM / TM / TCM to the Department of Regulation through PRAVAAH
Portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in) subject to fulfilment of the prudential
regulations as set out in paragraph 64 above.
requirements.object: of its Board, make an application for NOC for PCM / TM / TCM to the Department of Regulation through PRAVAAH Portal (https://pra

requirements.full_sentence:
The application shall also contain
the details of the proposed business plan as a TM / TCM / PCM along with
particulars of the risk management architecture instituted.
requirements.object: of the proposed business plan as a TM / TCM / PCM along with particulars of the risk management architecture instituted

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank acting as a TM / TCM / SCC of IIBX shall be required to seek additional
approval from the Department of Regulation in case of any change in their role
or scope of activities at IIBX from those permitted by this Direction.
requirements.object: additional approval from the Department of Regulation in case of any change in their role or scope of activities at IIBX from thos

requirements.full_sentence:
An Indian bank, including those operating in International Financial Services
Centers (IFSCs) in India such as Gujarat International Finance Tec City (GIFT
City), shall comply with the following directions regarding dealing in financial
derivative products by its foreign branches / subsidiaries.
requirements.object: directions regarding dealing in financial derivative products by its foreign branches / subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence:
The branches / subsidiaries of an Indian bank operating in IFSCs including those
operating out of GIFT City may also deal in financial derivative products,
<image>
including structured financial derivative products, which are not available or are
not permitted by the RBI in the domestic market subject to compliance with all
applicable laws / regulations and conditions stipulated in paragraph 78 below
and those prescribed by the host regulator.
requirements.object: derivative products, <image> including structured financial derivative products, which are not available or are not permitted b

requirements.full_sentence:
While allowing branches / subsidiaries in foreign jurisdictions as well as in IFSCs
to deal in such products, the parent Indian bank shall ensure that:
(1) dealing in such products is done with the prior approval from their Board
and, if required, the appropriate authority in the concerned jurisdictions;
requirements.object: in such products is done with the prior approval from their Board and
in such financial products as may be specified by the RBI in the manner and format and within the time frame as pres

requirements.full_sentence:
The financial products dealt with by the foreign branches and subsidiaries as
well as IFSCs shall attract the prudential norms such as capital adequacy,
exposure norms (including Large Exposure Framework), periodical valuation,
and all other applicable norms.
requirements.object: such as capital adequacy, exposure norms (including Large Exposure Framework), periodical valuation, and all other applic

requirements.full_sentence:
Parent bank shall adhere to more stringent
among the host and home regulations in respect of prudential norms.
requirements.object: among the host and home regulations in respect of prudential norms

conditions:
In case the current norms of the RBI do not specify prudential treatment of any
financial product
requirements.full_sentence:
In case the current norms of the RBI do not specify prudential treatment of any
financial product, the parent bank shall seek specific guidance from RBI.
requirements.object: RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The activities of branches / subsidiaries in foreign jurisdictions and IFSCs shall
be subject to the laws in India, unless specifically exempted by law.
requirements.object: laws in India

requirements.full_sentence:
The
directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may
be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or
punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or
guidelines had not been repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
<image>
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of
any provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
